NKJV.1: At that time Herod the tetrarch heard the report about Jesus
NKJV.2: and said to his servants, “This is John the Baptist; he is risen from the dead, and therefore these powers are at work in him.”
NKJV.3: For Herod had laid hold of John and bound him, and put him in prison for the sake of Herodias, his brother Philip’s wife.
NKJV.4: Because John had said to him, “It is not lawful for you to have her.”
NKJV.5: And although he wanted to put him to death, he feared the multitude, because they counted him as a prophet.
NKJV.6: But when Herod’s birthday was celebrated, the daughter of Herodias danced before them and pleased Herod.
NKJV.7: Therefore he promised with an oath to give her whatever she might ask.
NKJV.8: So she, having been prompted by her mother, said, “Give me John the Baptist’s head here on a platter.”
NKJV.9: And the king was sorry; nevertheless, because of the oaths and because of those who sat with him, he commanded it to be given to her.
NKJV.10: So he sent and had John beheaded in prison.
NKJV.11: And his head was brought on a platter and given to the girl, and she brought it to her mother.
NKJV.12: Then his disciples came and took away the body and buried it, and went and told Jesus.
NKJV.13: When Jesus heard it, He departed from there by boat to a deserted place by Himself. But when the multitudes heard it, they followed Him on foot from the cities.
NKJV.14: And when Jesus went out He saw a great multitude; and He was moved with compassion for them, and healed their sick.
NKJV.15: When it was evening, His disciples came to Him, saying, “This is a deserted place, and the hour is already late. Send the multitudes away, that they may go into the villages and buy themselves food.”
NKJV.16: But Jesus said to them, “They do not need to go away. You give them something to eat.”
NKJV.17: And they said to Him, “We have here only five loaves and two fish.”
NKJV.18: He said, “Bring them here to Me.”
NKJV.19: Then He commanded the multitudes to sit down on the grass. And He took the five loaves and the two fish, and looking up to heaven, He blessed and broke and gave the loaves to the disciples; and the disciples gave to the multitudes.
NKJV.20: So they all ate and were filled, and they took up twelve baskets full of the fragments that remained.
NKJV.21: Now those who had eaten were about five thousand men, besides women and children.
NKJV.22: Immediately Jesus made His disciples get into the boat and go before Him to the other side, while He sent the multitudes away.
NKJV.23: And when He had sent the multitudes away, He went up on the mountain by Himself to pray. Now when evening came, He was alone there.
NKJV.24: But the boat was now in the middle of the sea, tossed by the waves, for the wind was contrary.
NKJV.25: Now in the fourth watch of the night Jesus went to them, walking on the sea.
NKJV.26: And when the disciples saw Him walking on the sea, they were troubled, saying, “It is a ghost!” And they cried out for fear.
NKJV.27: But immediately Jesus spoke to them, saying, “Be of good  cheer! It is I; do not be afraid.”
NKJV.28: And Peter answered Him and said, “Lord, if it is You, command me to come to You on the water.”
NKJV.29: So He said, “Come.” And when Peter had come down out of the boat, he walked on the water to go to Jesus.
NKJV.30: But when he saw that the wind was boisterous, he was afraid; and beginning to sink he cried out, saying, “Lord, save me!”
NKJV.31: And immediately Jesus stretched out His hand and caught him, and said to him, “O you of little faith, why did you doubt?”
NKJV.32: And when they got into the boat, the wind ceased.
NKJV.33: Then those who were in the boat came and worshiped Him, saying, “Truly You are the Son of God.”
NKJV.34: When they had crossed over, they came to the land of Gennesaret.
NKJV.35: And when the men of that place recognized Him, they sent out into all that surrounding region, brought to Him all who were sick,
NKJV.36: and begged Him that they might only touch the hem of His garment. And as many as touched it were made perfectly well.

ESV.1: At that time Herod the tetrarch heard about the fame of Jesus,
ESV.2: and he said to his servants, “This is John the Baptist. He has been raised from the dead; that is why these miraculous powers are at work in him.”
ESV.3: For Herod had seized John and bound him and put him in prison for the sake of Herodias, his brother Philip's wife,
ESV.4: because John had been saying to him, “It is not lawful for you to have her.”
ESV.5: And though he wanted to put him to death, he feared the people, because they held him to be a prophet.
ESV.6: But when Herod's birthday came, the daughter of Herodias danced before the company and pleased Herod,
ESV.7: so that he promised with an oath to give her whatever she might ask.
ESV.8: Prompted by her mother, she said, “Give me the head of John the Baptist here on a platter.”
ESV.9: And the king was sorry, but because of his oaths and his guests he commanded it to be given.
ESV.10: He sent and had John beheaded in the prison,
ESV.11: and his head was brought on a platter and given to the girl, and she brought it to her mother.
ESV.12: And his disciples came and took the body and buried it, and they went and told Jesus.
ESV.13: Now when Jesus heard this, he withdrew from there in a boat to a desolate place by himself. But when the crowds heard it, they followed him on foot from the towns.
ESV.14: When he went ashore he saw a great crowd, and he had compassion on them and healed their sick.
ESV.15: Now when it was evening, the disciples came to him and said, “This is a desolate place, and the day is now over; send the crowds away to go into the villages and buy food for themselves.”
ESV.16: But Jesus said, “They need not go away; you give them something to eat.”
ESV.17: They said to him, “We have only five loaves here and two fish.”
ESV.18: And he said, “Bring them here to me.”
ESV.19: Then he ordered the crowds to sit down on the grass, and taking the five loaves and the two fish, he looked up to heaven and said a blessing. Then he broke the loaves and gave them to the disciples, and the disciples gave them to the crowds.
ESV.20: And they all ate and were satisfied. And they took up twelve baskets full of the broken pieces left over.
ESV.21: And those who ate were about five thousand men, besides women and children.
ESV.22: Immediately he made the disciples get into the boat and go before him to the other side, while he dismissed the crowds.
ESV.23: And after he had dismissed the crowds, he went up on the mountain by himself to pray. When evening came, he was there alone,
ESV.24: but the boat by this time was a long way  from the land,  beaten by the waves, for the wind was against them.
ESV.25: And in the fourth watch of the night  he came to them, walking on the sea.
ESV.26: But when the disciples saw him walking on the sea, they were terrified, and said, “It is a ghost!” and they cried out in fear.
ESV.27: But immediately Jesus spoke to them, saying,  “Take heart; it is I. Do not be afraid.”
ESV.28: And Peter answered him, “Lord, if it is you, command me to come to you on the water.”
ESV.29: He said, “Come.” So Peter got out of the boat and walked on the water and came to Jesus.
ESV.30: But when he saw the wind,  he was afraid, and beginning to sink he cried out, “Lord, save me.”
ESV.31: Jesus immediately reached out his hand and took hold of him, saying to him,  “O you of little faith, why did you doubt?”
ESV.32: And when they got into the boat, the wind ceased.
ESV.33: And those in the boat worshiped him, saying, “Truly you are the Son of God.”
ESV.34: And when they had crossed over, they came to land at Gennesaret.
ESV.35: And when the men of that place recognized him, they sent around to all that region and brought to him all who were sick
ESV.36: and implored him that they might only touch the fringe of his garment. And as many as touched it were made well.

NASB.1: At that time Herod the tetrarch heard the news about Jesus,
NASB.2: and said to his servants, 'This is John the Baptist; he has risen from the dead, and that is why miraculous powers are at work in him.'
NASB.3: For when Herod had John arrested, he bound him and put him in prison because of Herodias, the wife of his brother Philip.
NASB.4: For John had been saying to him, 'It is not lawful for you to have her.'
NASB.5: Although Herod wanted to put him to death, he feared the crowd, because they regarded John as a prophet.
NASB.6: But when Herod's birthday came, the daughter of Herodias danced before [them] and pleased Herod,
NASB.7: so [much] that he promised with an oath to give her whatever she asked.
NASB.8: Having been prompted by her mother, she said, 'Give me here on a platter the head of John the Baptist.'
NASB.9: Although he was grieved, the king commanded [it] to be given because of his oaths, and because of his dinner guests.
NASB.10: He sent and had John beheaded in the prison.
NASB.11: And his head was brought on a platter and given to the girl, and she brought it to her mother.
NASB.12: His disciples came and took away the body and buried it; and they went and reported to Jesus.
NASB.13: Now when Jesus heard [about John], He withdrew from there in a boat to a secluded place by Himself; and when the people heard [of this], they followed Him on foot from the cities.
NASB.14: When He went ashore, He saw a large crowd, and felt compassion for them and healed their sick.
NASB.15: When it was evening, the disciples came to Him and said, 'This place is desolate and the hour is already late; so send the crowds away, that they may go into the villages and buy food for themselves.'
NASB.16: But Jesus said to them, 'They do not need to go away; you give them [something] to eat!'
NASB.17: They said to Him, 'We have here only five loaves and two fish.'
NASB.18: And He said, 'Bring them here to Me.'
NASB.19: Ordering the people to sit down on the grass, He took the five loaves and the two fish, and looking up toward heaven, He blessed [the food], and breaking the loaves He gave them to the disciples, and the disciples [gave them] to the crowds,
NASB.20: and they all ate and were satisfied. They picked up what was left over of the broken pieces, twelve full baskets.
NASB.21: There were about five thousand men who ate, besides women and children.
NASB.22: Immediately He made the disciples get into the boat and go ahead of Him to the other side, while He sent the crowds away.
NASB.23: After He had sent the crowds away, He went up on the mountain by Himself to pray; and when it was evening, He was there alone.
NASB.24: But the boat was already a long distance from the land, battered by the waves; for the wind was contrary.
NASB.25: And in the fourth watch of the night He came to them, walking on the sea.
NASB.26: When the disciples saw Him walking on the sea, they were terrified, and said, 'It is a ghost!' And they cried out in fear.
NASB.27: But immediately Jesus spoke to them, saying, 'Take courage, it is I; do not be afraid.'
NASB.28: Peter said to Him, 'Lord, if it is You, command me to come to You on the water.'
NASB.29: And He said, 'Come!' And Peter got out of the boat, and walked on the water and came toward Jesus.
NASB.30: But seeing the wind, he became frightened, and beginning to sink, he cried out, 'Lord, save me!'
NASB.31: Immediately Jesus stretched out His hand and took hold of him, and said to him, 'You of little faith, why did you doubt?'
NASB.32: When they got into the boat, the wind stopped.
NASB.33: And those who were in the boat worshiped Him, saying, 'You are certainly God's Son!'
NASB.34: When they had crossed over, they came to land at Gennesaret.
NASB.35: And when the men of that place recognized Him, they sent [word] into all that surrounding district and brought to Him all who were sick;
NASB.36: and they implored Him that they might just touch the fringe of His cloak; and as many as touched [it] were cured.

NLT.1: When Herod Antipas, the ruler of Galilee, heard about Jesus,
NLT.2: he said to his advisers, “This must be John the Baptist raised from the dead! That is why he can do such miracles.”
NLT.3: For Herod had arrested and imprisoned John as a favor to his wife Herodias (the former wife of Herod’s brother Philip).
NLT.4: John had been telling Herod, “It is against God’s law for you to marry her.”
NLT.5: Herod wanted to kill John, but he was afraid of a riot, because all the people believed John was a prophet.
NLT.6: But at a birthday party for Herod, Herodias’s daughter performed a dance that greatly pleased him,
NLT.7: so he promised with a vow to give her anything she wanted.
NLT.8: At her mother’s urging, the girl said, “I want the head of John the Baptist on a tray!”
NLT.9: Then the king regretted what he had said; but because of the vow he had made in front of his guests, he issued the necessary orders.
NLT.10: So John was beheaded in the prison,
NLT.11: and his head was brought on a tray and given to the girl, who took it to her mother.
NLT.12: Later, John’s disciples came for his body and buried it. Then they went and told Jesus what had happened.
NLT.13: As soon as Jesus heard the news, he left in a boat to a remote area to be alone. But the crowds heard where he was headed and followed on foot from many towns.
NLT.14: Jesus saw the huge crowd as he stepped from the boat, and he had compassion on them and healed their sick.
NLT.15: That evening the disciples came to him and said, “This is a remote place, and it’s already getting late. Send the crowds away so they can go to the villages and buy food for themselves.”
NLT.16: But Jesus said, “That isn’t necessary — you feed them.”
NLT.17: “But we have only five loaves of bread and two fish!” they answered.
NLT.19: Then he told the people to sit down on the grass. Jesus took the five loaves and two fish, looked up toward heaven, and blessed them. Then, breaking the loaves into pieces, he gave the bread to the disciples, who distributed it to the people.
NLT.20: They all ate as much as they wanted, and afterward, the disciples picked up twelve baskets of leftovers.
NLT.21: About 5,000 men were fed that day, in addition to all the women and children!
NLT.22: Immediately after this, Jesus insisted that his disciples get back into the boat and cross to the other side of the lake, while he sent the people home.
NLT.23: After sending them home, he went up into the hills by himself to pray. Night fell while he was there alone.
NLT.24: Meanwhile, the disciples were in trouble far away from land, for a strong wind had risen, and they were fighting heavy waves.
NLT.25: About three o’clock in the morning Jesus came toward them, walking on the water.
NLT.26: When the disciples saw him walking on the water, they were terrified. In their fear, they cried out, “It’s a ghost!”
NLT.27: But Jesus spoke to them at once. “Don’t be afraid,” he said. “Take courage. I am here! ”
NLT.28: Then Peter called to him, “Lord, if it’s really you, tell me to come to you, walking on the water.”
NLT.29: “Yes, come,” Jesus said. So Peter went over the side of the boat and walked on the water toward Jesus.
NLT.30: But when he saw the strong wind and the waves, he was terrified and began to sink. “Save me, Lord!” he shouted.
NLT.31: Jesus immediately reached out and grabbed him. “You have so little faith,” Jesus said. “Why did you doubt me?”
NLT.32: When they climbed back into the boat, the wind stopped.
NLT.33: Then the disciples worshiped him. “You really are the Son of God!” they exclaimed.
NLT.34: After they had crossed the lake, they landed at Gennesaret.
NLT.35: When the people recognized Jesus, the news of his arrival spread quickly throughout the whole area, and soon people were bringing all their sick to be healed.
NLT.36: They begged him to let the sick touch at least the fringe of his robe, and all who touched him were healed.

WEB.1: At that time, Herod the tetrarch heard the report concerning Jesus,
WEB.2: and said to his servants, “This is John the Baptizer. He is risen from the dead. That is why these powers work in him.”
WEB.3: For Herod had laid hold of John, and bound him, and put him in prison for the sake of Herodias, his brother Philip’s wife.
WEB.4: For John said to him, “It is not lawful for you to have her.”
WEB.5: When he would have put him to death, he feared the multitude, because they counted him as a prophet.
WEB.6: But when Herod’s birthday came, the daughter of Herodias danced among them and pleased Herod.
WEB.7: Whereupon he promised with an oath to give her whatever she should ask.
WEB.8: She, being prompted by her mother, said, “Give me here on a platter the head of John the Baptizer.”
WEB.9: The king was grieved, but for the sake of his oaths, and of those who sat at the table with him, he commanded it to be given,
WEB.10: and he sent and beheaded John in the prison.
WEB.11: His head was brought on a platter, and given to the young lady: and she brought it to her mother.
WEB.12: His disciples came, and took the body, and buried it; and they went and told Jesus.
WEB.13: Now when Jesus heard this, he withdrew from there in a boat, to a deserted place apart. When the multitudes heard it, they followed him on foot from the cities.
WEB.14: Jesus went out, and he saw a great multitude. He had compassion on them, and healed their sick.
WEB.15: When evening had come, his disciples came to him, saying, “This place is deserted, and the hour is already late. Send the multitudes away, that they may go into the villages, and buy themselves food.”
WEB.16: But Jesus said to them, “They don’t need to go away. You give them something to eat.”
WEB.17: They told him, “We only have here five loaves and two fish.”
WEB.18: He said, “Bring them here to me.”
WEB.19: He commanded the multitudes to sit down on the grass; and he took the five loaves and the two fish, and looking up to heaven, he blessed, broke and gave the loaves to the disciples, and the disciples gave to the multitudes.
WEB.20: They all ate, and were filled. They took up twelve baskets full of that which remained left over from the broken pieces.
WEB.21: Those who ate were about five thousand men, besides women and children.
WEB.22: Immediately Jesus made the disciples get into the boat, and to go ahead of him to the other side, while he sent the multitudes away.
WEB.23: After he had sent the multitudes away, he went up into the mountain by himself to pray. When evening had come, he was there alone.
WEB.24: But the boat was now in the middle of the sea, distressed by the waves, for the wind was contrary.
WEB.25: In the fourth watch of the night,  Jesus came to them, walking on the sea.
WEB.26: When the disciples saw him walking on the sea, they were troubled, saying, “It’s a ghost!” and they cried out for fear.
WEB.27: But immediately Jesus spoke to them, saying, “Cheer up! It is I!   Don’t be afraid.”
WEB.28: Peter answered him and said, “Lord, if it is you, command me to come to you on the waters.”
WEB.29: He said, “Come!” Peter stepped down from the boat, and walked on the waters to come to Jesus.
WEB.30: But when he saw that the wind was strong, he was afraid, and beginning to sink, he cried out, saying, “Lord, save me!”
WEB.31: Immediately Jesus stretched out his hand, took hold of him, and said to him, “You of little faith, why did you doubt?”
WEB.32: When they got up into the boat, the wind ceased.
WEB.33: Those who were in the boat came and worshiped him, saying, “You are truly the Son of God!”
WEB.34: When they had crossed over, they came to the land of Gennesaret.
WEB.35: When the people of that place recognized him, they sent into all that surrounding region, and brought to him all who were sick;
WEB.36: and they begged him that they might just touch the fringe  of his garment. As many as touched it were made whole.

CSB17.1: At that time <sup>ⓝ</sup> Herod the tetrarch <sup>ⓐ</sup> heard the report about Jesus.
CSB17.2: “This is John the Baptist,” he told his servants. “He has been raised from the dead, and that’s why miraculous powers are at work in him.” <sup>ⓑ</sup>
CSB17.3: For Herod had arrested John, chained <sup>[1]</sup> him, and put him in prison on account of Herodias, his brother Philip’s wife, <sup>ⓒ</sup>
CSB17.4: since John had been telling him, “It’s not lawful <sup>ⓓ</sup> for you to have her.”
CSB17.5: Though Herod wanted to kill John, he feared the crowd since they regarded John as a prophet. <sup>ⓔ</sup>
CSB17.6: When Herod’s birthday celebration came, Herodias’s daughter danced before them <sup>[2]</sup> and pleased Herod.
CSB17.7: So he promised with an oath to give her whatever she asked.
CSB17.8: Prompted by her mother, she answered, “Give me John the Baptist’s head here on a platter.”
CSB17.9: Although the king regretted it, he commanded that it be granted because of his oaths and his guests.
CSB17.10: So he sent orders and had John beheaded in the prison.
CSB17.11: His head was brought on a platter and given to the girl, who carried it to her mother.
CSB17.12: Then his disciples came, removed the corpse, <sup>[3]</sup> buried it, and went and reported to Jesus.
CSB17.13: When Jesus heard about it, <sup>ⓕ</sup> he withdrew from there by boat to a remote place to be alone. When the crowds heard this, they followed him on foot from the towns.
CSB17.14: When he went ashore, <sup>[4]</sup> he saw a large crowd, had compassion on them, and healed their sick. <sup>ⓖ</sup>
CSB17.15: When evening came, the disciples <sup>ⓗ</sup> approached him and said, “This place is deserted, and it is already late. <sup>[5]</sup> Send the crowds away so that they can go into the villages and buy food for themselves.”
CSB17.16: “They don’t need to go away,” Jesus told them. “You give them something to eat.”
CSB17.17: “But we only have five loaves and two fish here,” they said to him.
CSB17.18: “Bring them here to me,” he said.
CSB17.19: Then he commanded the crowds to sit down on the grass. He took the five loaves and the two fish, and looking up to heaven, he blessed them. He broke the loaves and gave them to the disciples, and the disciples gave them to the crowds. <sup>ⓘ</sup>
CSB17.20: Everyone ate and was satisfied. They picked up twelve baskets full of leftover pieces. <sup>ⓙ</sup>
CSB17.21: Now those who ate were about five thousand men, besides women and children.
CSB17.22: Immediately <sup>ⓚ</sup> he <sup>[6]</sup> made the disciples get into the boat and go ahead of him to the other side, while he dismissed the crowds.
CSB17.23: After dismissing the crowds, he went up on the mountain by himself to pray. Well into the night, he was there alone. <sup>ⓛ</sup>
CSB17.24: Meanwhile, the boat was already some distance <sup>[7]</sup> from land, <sup>[8]</sup> battered by the waves, because the wind was against them.
CSB17.25: Jesus came toward them walking on the sea <sup>ⓜ</sup> very early in the morning. <sup>[9]</sup>
CSB17.26: When the disciples saw him walking on the sea, they were terrified. “It’s a ghost!” they said, and they cried out in fear.
CSB17.27: Immediately Jesus spoke to them. “Have courage! It is I. Don’t be afraid.” <sup>ⓝ</sup>
CSB17.28: “Lord, if it’s you,” Peter answered him, “command me to come to you on the water.”
CSB17.29: He said, “Come.” And climbing out of the boat, Peter started walking on the water and came toward Jesus.
CSB17.30: But when he saw the strength of the wind, <sup>[10]</sup> he was afraid, and beginning to sink he cried out, “Lord, save me!”
CSB17.31: Immediately Jesus reached out his hand, caught hold of him, and said to him, “You of little faith, why did you doubt?” <sup>ⓐ</sup>
CSB17.32: When they got into the boat, the wind ceased.
CSB17.33: Then those in the boat worshiped him and said, “Truly you are the Son of God.” <sup>ⓑ</sup>
CSB17.34: When they had crossed over, <sup>ⓒ</sup> they came to shore at Gennesaret. <sup>ⓓ</sup>
CSB17.35: When the men of that place recognized him, they alerted the whole vicinity and brought to him all who were sick.
CSB17.36: They begged him that they might only touch the end of his robe, and as many as touched it were healed. <sup>ⓔ</sup>

AMP.1: At that time Herod [Antipas], the tetrarch [who governed a portion of Palestine including Galilee and Perea], heard the reports about Jesus,
AMP.2: and said to his attendants, "This is John the Baptist; he has been raised from the dead, and that is why the miraculous powers are at work in him."
AMP.3: For Herod had John arrested and bound him and put him in prison [at the fortress of Machaerus, east of the Jordan, to keep him away] because of Herodias, the wife of his brother Philip,
AMP.4: for John had said to him, "It is not lawful (morally right) for you to have her [living with you as your wife]."
AMP.5: Although Herod wished to have him put to death, he feared the people, for they regarded John as a prophet.
AMP.6: But when Herod's birthday came, [his niece Salome], the daughter of Herodias danced [immodestly] before them and pleased and fascinated Herod,
AMP.7: so much that he promised with an oath to give her whatever she asked.
AMP.8: She, being coached by her mother [Herodias], said, "Give me here on a platter the head of John the Baptist."
AMP.9: The king was distressed, but because of his oaths, and because of his dinner guests, he ordered it to be given her.
AMP.10: He sent and had John beheaded in the prison.
AMP.11: His head was brought on a platter and given to the girl, and she brought it to her mother [Herodias].
AMP.12: And John's disciples came and took away the body and buried it. Then they went and told Jesus.
AMP.13: When Jesus heard about John, He left there privately in a boat and went to a secluded place. But when the crowds heard of this, they followed Him on foot from the cities.
AMP.14: When He went ashore, He saw a large crowd, and felt [profound] compassion for them and healed their sick.
AMP.15: When evening came, the disciples came to Him and said, "This is an isolated place and the hour is already late; send the crowds away so that they may go into the villages and buy food for themselves."
AMP.16: But Jesus said to them, "They do not need to go away; you give them something to eat!"
AMP.17: They replied, "We have nothing here except five loaves and two fish."
AMP.18: He said, "Bring them here to Me."
AMP.19: Then He ordered the crowds to sit down on the grass, and He took the five loaves and the two fish and, looking up toward heaven, He blessed and broke the loaves and gave them to the disciples, and the disciples gave them to the people,
AMP.20: and they all ate and were satisfied. They picked up twelve full baskets of the leftover broken pieces.
AMP.21: There were about 5,000 men who ate, besides women and children.
AMP.22: Immediately He directed the disciples to get into the boat and go ahead of Him to the other side [of the Sea of Galilee], while He sent the crowds away.
AMP.23: After He had dismissed the crowds, He went up on the mountain by Himself to pray. When it was evening, He was there alone.
AMP.24: But the boat [by this time] was already a long distance from land, tossed and battered by the waves; for the wind was against them.
AMP.25: And in the fourth watch of the night (3:00-6:00 a.m.) Jesus came to them, walking on the sea.
AMP.26: When the disciples saw Him walking on the sea, they were terrified, and said, "It is a ghost!" And they cried out in fear.
AMP.27: But immediately He spoke to them, saying, "Take courage, it is I! Do not be afraid!"
AMP.28: Peter replied to Him, "Lord, if it is [really] You, command me to come to You on the water."
AMP.29: He said, "Come!" So Peter got out of the boat, and walked on the water and came toward Jesus.
AMP.30: But when he saw [the effects of] the wind, he was frightened, and he began to sink, and he cried out, "Lord, save me!"
AMP.31: Immediately Jesus extended His hand and caught him, saying to him, "O you of little faith, why did you doubt?"
AMP.32: And when they got into the boat, the wind ceased.
AMP.33: Then those in the boat worshiped Him [with awe-inspired reverence], saying, "Truly You are the Son of God!"
AMP.34: When they had crossed over [the sea], they went ashore at Gennesaret.
AMP.35: And when the men of that place recognized Him, they sent word throughout all the surrounding district and brought to Him all who were sick;
AMP.36: and they begged Him to let them merely touch the fringe of His robe; and all who touched it were perfectly restored.

MSG.1: At about this time, Herod, the regional ruler, heard what was being said about Jesus.
MSG.2: He said to his servants, "This has to be John the Baptizer come back from the dead. That's why he's able to work miracles!"
MSG.3: Herod had arrested John, put him in chains, and sent him to prison to placate Herodias, his brother Philip's wife.
MSG.4: John had provoked Herod by naming his relationship with Herodias "adultery."
MSG.5: Herod wanted to kill him, but he was afraid because so many people revered John as a prophet of God.
MSG.6: But at his birthday celebration, he got his chance. Herodias's daughter provided the entertainment, dancing for the guests. She swept Herod away.
MSG.7: In his drunken enthusiasm, he promised her on oath anything she wanted.
MSG.8: Already coached by her mother, she was ready: "Give me, served up on a platter, the head of John the Baptizer."
MSG.9: That sobered the king up fast. Unwilling to lose face with his guests, he did it
MSG.11: and presented to the girl on a platter. She in turn gave it to her mother.
MSG.12: Later, John's disciples got the body, gave it a reverent burial, and reported to Jesus.
MSG.13: When Jesus got the news, he slipped away by boat to an out-of-the-way place by himself. But unsuccessfully—someone saw him and the word got around. Soon a lot of people from the nearby villages walked around the lake to where he was.
MSG.14: When he saw them coming, he was overcome with pity and healed their sick.
MSG.15: Toward evening the disciples approached him. "We're out in the country and it's getting late. Dismiss the people so they can go to the villages and get some supper."
MSG.16: But Jesus said, "There is no need to dismiss them. You give them supper."
MSG.17: "All we have are five loaves of bread and two fish," they said.
MSG.19: Then he had the people sit on the grass. He took the five loaves and two fish, lifted his face to heaven in prayer, blessed, broke, and gave the bread to the disciples. The disciples then gave the food to the congregation.
MSG.20: They all ate their fill. They gathered twelve baskets of leftovers.
MSG.22: As soon as the meal was finished, he insisted that the disciples get in the boat and go on ahead to the other side while he dismissed the people.
MSG.23: With the crowd dispersed, he climbed the mountain so he could be by himself and pray. He stayed there alone, late into the night.
MSG.24: Meanwhile, the boat was far out to sea when the wind came up against them and they were battered by the waves.
MSG.25: At about four o'clock in the morning, Jesus came toward them walking on the water.
MSG.26: They were scared out of their wits. "A ghost!" they said, crying out in terror.
MSG.27: But Jesus was quick to comfort them. "Courage, it's me. Don't be afraid."
MSG.28: Peter, suddenly bold, said, "Master, if it's really you, call me to come to you on the water."
MSG.29: He said, "Come ahead." Jumping out of the boat, Peter walked on the water to Jesus.
MSG.30: But when he looked down at the waves churning beneath his feet, he lost his nerve and started to sink. He cried, "Master, save me!"
MSG.31: Jesus didn't hesitate. He reached down and grabbed his hand. Then he said, "Faint-heart, what got into you?"
MSG.32: The two of them climbed into the boat, and the wind died down.
MSG.33: The disciples in the boat, having watched the whole thing, worshiped Jesus, saying, "This is it! You are God's Son for sure!"
MSG.34: On return, they beached the boat at Gennesaret.
MSG.35: When the people got wind that he was back, they sent out word through the neighborhood and rounded up all the sick,
MSG.36: who asked for permission to touch the edge of his coat. And whoever touched him was healed.

NIV.1:
John the Baptist Beheaded
At that time Herod the tetrarch heard the reports about Jesus,
NIV.2: and he said to his attendants, “This is John the Baptist; he has risen from the dead! That is why miraculous powers are at work in him.”
NIV.3: Now Herod had arrested John and bound him and put him in prison because of Herodias, his brother Philip’s wife,
NIV.4: for John had been saying to him: “It is not lawful for you to have her.”
NIV.5: Herod wanted to kill John, but he was afraid of the people, because they considered him a prophet.
NIV.6: On Herod’s birthday the daughter of Herodias danced for them and pleased Herod so much
NIV.7: that he promised with an oath to give her whatever she asked.
NIV.8: Prompted by her mother, she said, “Give me here on a platter the head of John the Baptist.”
NIV.9: The king was distressed, but because of his oaths and his dinner guests, he ordered that her request be granted
NIV.10: and had John beheaded in the prison.
NIV.11: His head was brought in on a platter and given to the girl, who carried it to her mother.
NIV.12: John’s disciples came and took his body and buried it. Then they went and told Jesus.
NIV.13:
Jesus Feeds the Five Thousand
When Jesus heard what had happened, he withdrew by boat privately to a solitary place. Hearing of this, the crowds followed him on foot from the towns.
NIV.14: When Jesus landed and saw a large crowd, he had compassion on them and healed their sick.
NIV.15: As evening approached, the disciples came to him and said, “This is a remote place, and it’s already getting late. Send the crowds away, so they can go to the villages and buy themselves some food.”
NIV.16: Jesus replied, “They do not need to go away. You give them something to eat.”
NIV.17: “We have here only five loaves of bread and two fish,” they answered.
NIV.18: “Bring them here to me,” he said.
NIV.19: And he directed the people to sit down on the grass. Taking the five loaves and the two fish and looking up to heaven, he gave thanks and broke the loaves. Then he gave them to the disciples, and the disciples gave them to the people.
NIV.20: They all ate and were satisfied, and the disciples picked up twelve basketfuls of broken pieces that were left over.
NIV.21: The number of those who ate was about five thousand men, besides women and children.
NIV.22:
Jesus Walks on the Water
Immediately Jesus made the disciples get into the boat and go on ahead of him to the other side, while he dismissed the crowd.
NIV.23: After he had dismissed them, he went up on a mountainside by himself to pray. When evening came, he was there alone,
NIV.24: but the boat was already a considerable distance from land, buffeted by the waves because the wind was against it.
NIV.25: During the fourth watch of the night Jesus went out to them, walking on the lake.
NIV.26: When the disciples saw him walking on the lake, they were terrified. “It’s a ghost,” they said, and cried out in fear.
NIV.27: But Jesus immediately said to them: “Take courage! It is I. Don’t be afraid.”
NIV.28: “Lord, if it’s you,” Peter replied, “tell me to come to you on the water.”
NIV.29:
“Come,” he said.
Then Peter got down out of the boat, walked on the water and came toward Jesus.
NIV.30: But when he saw the wind, he was afraid and, beginning to sink, cried out, “Lord, save me!”
NIV.31: Immediately Jesus reached out his hand and caught him. “You of little faith,” he said, “why did you doubt?”
NIV.32: And when they climbed into the boat, the wind died down.
NIV.33: Then those who were in the boat worshiped him, saying, “Truly you are the Son of God.”
NIV.34: When they had crossed over, they landed at Gennesaret.
NIV.35: And when the men of that place recognized Jesus, they sent word to all the surrounding country. People brought all their sick to him
NIV.36: and begged him to let the sick just touch the edge of his cloak, and all who touched him were healed.

NRSVCE.1: At that time Herod the ruler heard reports about Jesus;
NRSVCE.2: and he said to his servants, “This is John the Baptist; he has been raised from the dead, and for this reason these powers are at work in him.”
NRSVCE.3: For Herod had arrested John, bound him, and put him in prison on account of Herodias, his brother Philip’s wife,
NRSVCE.4: because John had been telling him, “It is not lawful for you to have her.”
NRSVCE.5: Though Herod wanted to put him to death, he feared the crowd, because they regarded him as a prophet.
NRSVCE.6: But when Herod’s birthday came, the daughter of Herodias danced before the company, and she pleased Herod
NRSVCE.7: so much that he promised on oath to grant her whatever she might ask.
NRSVCE.8: Prompted by her mother, she said, “Give me the head of John the Baptist here on a platter.”
NRSVCE.9: The king was grieved, yet out of regard for his oaths and for the guests, he commanded it to be given;
NRSVCE.10: he sent and had John beheaded in the prison.

NRSVCE.12: His disciples came and took the body and buried it; then they went and told Jesus.

NRSVCE.15: When it was evening, the disciples came to him and said, “This is a deserted place, and the hour is now late; send the crowds away so that they may go into the villages and buy food for themselves.”
NRSVCE.16: Jesus said to them, “They need not go away; you give them something to eat.”
NRSVCE.17: They replied, “We have nothing here but five loaves and two fish.”
NRSVCE.18: And he said, “Bring them here to me.”
NRSVCE.19: Then he ordered the crowds to sit down on the grass. Taking the five loaves and the two fish, he looked up to heaven, and blessed and broke the loaves, and gave them to the disciples, and the disciples gave them to the crowds.
NRSVCE.20: And all ate and were filled; and they took up what was left over of the broken pieces, twelve baskets full.
NRSVCE.21: And those who ate were about five thousand men, besides women and children.
NRSVCE.22: Immediately he made the disciples get into the boat and go on ahead to the other side, while he dismissed the crowds.
NRSVCE.23: And after he had dismissed the crowds, he went up the mountain by himself to pray. When evening came, he was there alone,
NRSVCE.24: but by this time the boat, battered by the waves, was far from the land, for the wind was against them.
NRSVCE.25: And early in the morning he came walking toward them on the sea.
NRSVCE.26: But when the disciples saw him walking on the sea, they were terrified, saying, “It is a ghost!” And they cried out in fear.
NRSVCE.27: But immediately Jesus spoke to them and said, “Take heart, it is I; do not be afraid.”
NRSVCE.28: Peter answered him, “Lord, if it is you, command me to come to you on the water.”
NRSVCE.29: He said, “Come.” So Peter got out of the boat, started walking on the water, and came toward Jesus.
NRSVCE.30: But when he noticed the strong wind, he became frightened, and beginning to sink, he cried out, “Lord, save me!”
NRSVCE.31: Jesus immediately reached out his hand and caught him, saying to him, “You of little faith, why did you doubt?”
NRSVCE.32: When they got into the boat, the wind ceased.
NRSVCE.33: And those in the boat worshiped him, saying, “Truly you are the Son of God.”
NRSVCE.34: When they had crossed over, they came to land at Gennesaret.
NRSVCE.35: After the people of that place recognized him, they sent word throughout the region and brought all who were sick to him,
NRSVCE.36: and begged him that they might touch even the fringe of his cloak; and all who touched it were healed.

NET.1: At that time Herod the tetrarch heard reports about Jesus,
NET.2: and he said to his servants, “This is John the Baptist. He has been raised from the dead! And because of this, miraculous powers are at work in him.”
NET.3: For Herod had arrested John, bound him, and put him in prison on account of Herodias, his brother Philip’s wife,
NET.4: because John had repeatedly told him, “It is not lawful for you to have her.”
NET.5: Although Herod wanted to kill John, he feared the crowd because they accepted John as a prophet.
NET.6: But on Herod’s birthday, the daughter of Herodias danced before them and pleased Herod,
NET.7: so much that he promised with an oath to give her whatever she asked.
NET.8: Instructed by her mother, she said, “Give me the head of John the Baptist here on a platter.”
NET.9: Although it grieved the king, because of his oath and the dinner guests he commanded it to be given.
NET.10: So he sent and had John beheaded in the prison.
NET.11: His head was brought on a platter and given to the girl, and she brought it to her mother.
NET.12: Then John’s disciples came and took the body and buried it and went and told Jesus.
NET.13: Now when Jesus heard this he went away from there privately in a boat to an isolated place. But when the crowd heard about it, they followed him on foot from the towns.
NET.14: As he got out he saw the large crowd, and he had compassion on them and healed their sick.
NET.15: When evening arrived, his disciples came to him saying, “This is an isolated place and the hour is already late. Send the crowds away so that they can go into the villages and buy food for themselves.”
NET.16: But he replied, “They don’t need to go. You give them something to eat.”
NET.17: They said to him, “We have here only five loaves and two fish.”
NET.18: “Bring them here to me,” he replied.
NET.19: Then he instructed the crowds to sit down on the grass. He took the five loaves and two fish, and looking up to heaven he gave thanks and broke the loaves. He gave them to the disciples, who in turn gave them to the crowds.
NET.20: They all ate and were satisfied, and they picked up the broken pieces left over, twelve baskets full.
NET.21: Not counting women and children, there were about five thousand men who ate.
NET.22: Immediately Jesus made the disciples get into the boat and go ahead of him to the other side, while he dispersed the crowds.
NET.23: And after he sent the crowds away, he went up the mountain by himself to pray. When evening came, he was there alone.
NET.24: Meanwhile the boat, already far from land, was taking a beating from the waves because the wind was against it.
NET.25: As the night was ending, Jesus came to them walking on the sea.
NET.26: When the disciples saw him walking on the water they were terrified and said, “It’s a ghost!” and cried out with fear.
NET.27: But immediately Jesus spoke to them: “Have courage! It is I. Do not be afraid.”
NET.28: Peter said to him, “Lord, if it is you, order me to come to you on the water.”
NET.29: So he said, “Come.” Peter got out of the boat, walked on the water, and came toward Jesus.
NET.30: But when he saw the strong wind he became afraid. And starting to sink, he cried out, “Lord, save me!”
NET.31: Immediately Jesus reached out his hand and caught him, saying to him, “You of little faith, why did you doubt?”
NET.32: When they went up into the boat, the wind ceased.
NET.33: Then those who were in the boat worshiped him, saying, “Truly you are the Son of God.”
NET.34: After they had crossed over, they came to land at Gennesaret.
NET.35: When the people there recognized him, they sent word into all the surrounding area, and they brought all their sick to him.
NET.36: They begged him if they could only touch the edge of his cloak, and all who touched it were healed.
